highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.148.0 is the first stable release of the v4 line, built on Skia m148.
highlights_body: It brings variable font support, animated WebP encoding, and a reworked singleton lifecycle. This is a breaking release — review the behavioural changes below before upgrading.

breaking.title: SKPaint no longer exposes legacy text and font state
breaking.body: The paint text/font members obsoleted in v3 are now compile errors. Move typeface, text size and measurement onto SKFont.

breaking.title: Singleton lifecycle reworked
breaking.body: Cached singleton instances are now created and disposed differently; code that held references across app teardown should re-fetch them.

bullets.detail: Pulls in the latest upstream rendering fixes and the m148 API surface.
Picks up the latest security and correctness fixes in the bundled XML parser.
SKWebpEncoder can now write multi-frame animated WebP images.
SKStream.GetData() returns the stream contents without an extra copy.

bullets.lead: SKPixmap and SKBitmap pixel access corrected
bullets.detail: GetPixelSpan now uses RowBytes for stride and the correct axis for offsets.
Disposing an SKPathBuilder before its SKPath no longer crashes during finalization.
The projection DLL is found again for .NET 9 consumers.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Singleton lifecycle rework, promoting the obsolete paint APIs to errors, the SKPath finalizer fix and Uno sample updates
contributor_summaries.ebariche: Reduced the SkiaFiddle WASM sample size by roughly 60%
contributor_summaries.sasakrsmanovic: Updated the Uno Platform README link and description

preview_summaries.rc1: The release candidate finalised the Skia m148 upgrade, expat 2.8.1 and the singleton lifecycle rework.
preview_summaries.p3: Preview 3 added the HBColor struct, refreshed several native dependencies and expanded the sample gallery.
preview_summaries.p2: Preview 2 introduced animated WebP encoding, zero-copy stream-to-data and the SKPath finalizer fix.
preview_summaries.p1: Preview 1 opened the v4 line with the initial Skia milestone bump and API cleanup.
